channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: World Press Photo Contest winners cast a lens on resilience, pain and bliss
item.description: A selection of prize honorees from the 2026 World Press Photo Contest capture the pain of the past year — but also focus on moments of strength, determination and joy.
item.content:encoded:
A selection of prize honorees from the 2026 World Press Photo Contest capture the pain of the past year — but also focus on moments of strength, determination and joy.

(Image credit: Ihsaan Haffejee for GroundUp)
item.dc:creator: Kamala Thiagarajan

item.title: Trump doubts shooter motivated by Iran war as peace talks on hold
item.description: A shooting incident at the White House Correspondent's Dinner took focus away from the war in Iran, as Iran's foreign minister planned to return to Islamabad, the site of previous peace talks.
item.content:encoded:
A shooting incident at the White House Correspondent's Dinner took focus away from the war in Iran, as Iran's foreign minister planned to return to Islamabad, the site of previous peace talks.

(Image credit: Meysam Mirzadeh)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: Kenya's Sabastian Sawe is first person to run sub-2-hour marathon to win in London
item.description: In a huge moment in sports history, Sabastian Sawe smashed the men's world record by 65 seconds in winning the London Marathon in 1 hour, 59 minutes and 30 seconds on Sunday.
item.content:encoded:
In a huge moment in sports history, Sabastian Sawe smashed the men's world record by 65 seconds in winning the London Marathon in 1 hour, 59 minutes and 30 seconds on Sunday.

(Image credit: Ian Walton)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: King Charles U.S. visit comes at tense moment in transatlantic relationship
item.description: King Charles and Queen Camilla arrive Monday for a four day U.S. state visit. Some hope the royal touch can heal the transatlantic rift that's emerged under Trump.
item.content:encoded:
King Charles and Queen Camilla arrive Monday for a four day U.S. state visit. Some hope the royal touch can heal the transatlantic rift that's emerged under Trump.

(Image credit: Yui Mok/AP)
item.dc:creator: Fatima Al-Kassab

item.title: For students labeled 'emotionally disturbed,' separation can lead to isolation
item.description: Every school has problem students, but some are labeled emotionally disturbed (ED) and taught separately from others.
item.content:encoded:
Every school has problem students, but some are labeled emotionally disturbed (ED) and taught separately from others.

(Image credit: Yasmin Yassin for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Laurie Stern

item.title: The Supreme Court case that could redefine your digital privacy
item.description: Police in Virginia used a technique called geofencing to tap into Google's databases to find out who was near the scene of a bank robbery. The Supreme Court will consider whether it is constitutional.
item.content:encoded: Police in Virginia used a technique called geofencing to tap into Google's databases to find out who was near the scene of a bank robbery. The Supreme Court will consider whether it is constitutional.
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg

item.title: A suspect is in custody after Trump is rushed from correspondents' dinner
item.description: President Trump and several cabinet members were safely rushed from the event in Washington, D.C. after several loud sounds were heard. The Secret Service said one person was in custody.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump and several cabinet members were safely rushed from the event in Washington, D.C. after several loud sounds were heard. The Secret Service said one person was in custody.

(Image credit: Kent Nishimura)
item.dc:creator: Eric McDaniel

item.title: New CEO Steve O'Donnell vows to unite NASCAR and return the fun
item.description: Steve O'Donnell was introduced as the sanctioning body's chief executive officer at Talladega Superspeedway on Saturday and vowed to "make some moves" that will return the storied racing series to its roots.
item.content:encoded:
Steve O'Donnell was introduced as the sanctioning body's chief executive officer at Talladega Superspeedway on Saturday and vowed to "make some moves" that will return the storied racing series to its roots.

(Image credit: Mike McCarn)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Dirk Kempthorne, former Idaho governor and U.S. Interior secretary, dies at 74
item.description: Dirk Kempthorne, a Republican, was elected mayor of Boise at age 34 and served seven years before serving one term in the U.S. Senate and then as governor until 2006.
item.content:encoded:
Dirk Kempthorne, a Republican, was elected mayor of Boise at age 34 and served seven years before serving one term in the U.S. Senate and then as governor until 2006.

(Image credit: Manuel Balce Ceneta)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: In a rare interview, a leader of the world's largest right-wing group talks to NPR
item.description: The second-in-command of the RSS, a Hindu nationalist organization in India, rarely speaks to the Western press. Here's what he said about his group's controversial history.
item.content:encoded:
The second-in-command of the RSS, a Hindu nationalist organization in India, rarely speaks to the Western press. Here's what he said about his group's controversial history.

(Image credit: IDREES MOHAMMED)
item.dc:creator: Rob Schmitz
